title: Occupational Therapist

rationales.taskAutomation: Progress documentation and standard outcome-measure tracking are automatable; hands-on functional assessment and treatment are not.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI documentation and motion-tracking tools targeting rehab therapy are credible and improving, not yet standard equipment.
rationales.adoption: Larger outpatient and telehealth-enabled practices are adopting AI documentation tools; broader adoption remains early.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can generate home-program plans and track outcome measures, but hands-on functional treatment stays human.

salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 29-1122 (Occupational Therapists), p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI now handles documentation and tracks recovery metrics, but functional rehabilitation is inherently hands-on work built around a specific patient's body and environment. Demand keeps climbing alongside an aging population and rising chronic-condition rates.
